data.title: TMK Energy Sells 20% Stake in Australian Project; Shares Up 50%
data.description:
TMK Energy executed an agreement to sell its 20% non-operated indirect interest in the Talisman Deeps Project offshore Canarvon Basin, northwest Australia, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The company held an indirect interest in the project as it held a 20% stake in Skye Napoleon.

The company has consented to Skye Napoleon relinquishing its rights to Talisman Deeps in return for a deferred consideration to the company of AU$1 million.

The company intends to focus on the appraisal and development of the Gurvantes XXXV Coal Seam Gas project in Mongolia, and the sale of the non-operated Talisman Deeps Project provides an opportunity to potentially monetise the asset, the filing said.

The company's shares jumped 50% in recent Friday trade.

data.title: MAS to Streamline Process to Improve Singapore IPO Outreach
data.description:
The Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) has proposed improvements in its process for initial public offerings, aiming to smoothen the process for issuers, according to a media release on Thursday.

The proposals are focused on streamlining prospectus disclosures for IPOs, simplifying the process for secondary listings and to offer greater flexibility for issuers in order for them to attract investment at the early stages of the IPO process.

MAS has invited suggestions on the proposals by June 14, the release added.

data.title: Moody's Affirms Acea Rating on Receipt of New Contract for Waste-to-Energy Plant in Italy
data.description:
Moody's on Thursday affirmed Acea's (ACE.MI) Baa2 long-term issuer rating, citing expectations of a modest impact on the company's credit metrics of a recent contract to develop a new waste-to-energy plant in Italy.

A consortium led by the Italian utility company's Acea Ambiente subsidiary secured the contract for the project earlier in May. The winning consortium will also own the concession to operate the plant, with Acea's unit owning 57% of the project.

The outlook on the rating is stable, reflecting Moody's expectations that the company's financial metrics will continue to be in line with its rating.

data.title: Hastings Technology Metals sells 60% of Yangibana project to Wyloo in strategic JV agreement
data.description: Hastings Technology Metals has entered into a definitive agreement to sell a 60% interest in the Yangibana rare earths and niobium project to the Andrew Forrest-backed Wyloo Metals. The company has also agreed to form an unincorporated joint venture (JV) for the Yangibana project, which is located in the Gascoyne region of Western Australia. This follows Wyloo’s decision in February of this year to enter into exclusive negotiations with Hastings about forming the JV for Yangibana and acquiring its equity interest in TSX-listed Neo Performance Materials. Offtake agreement Hastings had previously signed a non-binding heads of agreement with Neo outlining the framework for the parties to negotiate a binding commercial offtake agreement for the supply of rare earth concentrate from Stage 1 of the Yangibana project. Now, upon completion of the transaction, Wyloo will be appointed as the manager of Yangibana to advance the project to a final investment decision (FID). As part of the agreement, Wyloo has also agreed to redeem all outstanding exchangeable notes Hastings was due to repay by October 2025, with an estimated face value of $135 million. Significant de-risking Hastings executive chair Charles Lew said the JV with Wyloo would significantly de-risk Yangibana and reduce the capital contribution required from Hastings’ shareholders, while enabling Hastings to maintain a meaningful interest in the project. “With substantial investment in supporting infrastructure having been completed at Yangibana, the JV will be well placed to move forward with the construction and development of a global Tier 1 rare earths and niobium project,” Mr Lew said. Once it has completed the transaction, Hastings will focus on its Ark Gold and Brockman niobium and heavy rare earths projects in WA. Key energy transition role Wyloo chief executive officer Luca Giacovazzi said his company would work with the Hastings team to transition the Yangibana project to the JV over the coming months. “As rare earths continue to play a key role in the energy transition, Yangibana will be well placed to become a globally significant supplier of green critical minerals,” Mr Giacovazzi said. “As one of the most advanced rare earths projects in the world, Yangibana will be able to produce concentrate within 18 months from a FID.”

data.title: BOJ Rate Hike Likely in First Quarter of 2026, SMBC Nikko Says — Market Talk
data.description: The Bank of Japan is likely to raise interest rates to 0.75% from the current 0.5% in 1Q of 2026 after it confirms momentum for the next year's wage talks, say economists at SMBC Nikko Securities. "The bank would consider additional rate hikes after confirming that the Japanese economy is on a sufficient wage increase trajectory and on track to achieve the 2% inflation target, even after tariffs are imposed," the economists say in a research note. Friday's data showed Japan's economy shrank in 1Q for the first time in a year. "As the bank isn't rushing interest-rate hikes, the economic contraction in the January-March quarter may not be of great significance," they add. The economists expect the BOJ to raise its policy rate to 1.0% in the 3Q of 2026. (megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com)

data.title: Bergman & Beving Buys Swedish Supplier of Hoses, Couplings
data.description:
Bergman & Beving (BERG-B.ST) said Thursday it purchased the Sweden-based hoses and couplings supplier, Mann & Co.

The Swedish investment company purchased the company through its portfolio company Germ. It said the transaction will boost its offer of fluid handling products.

Bergman & Beving integrated Mann & Co. into its industrial equipment division, with the transaction expected to have a marginal impact on its EPS.

data.title: Australia's Medallion Metals rises on securing funding for gold and copper project
data.description:
** Shares of Medallion Metals rise as much as 12.5% to A$0.27, their biggest intraday pct gains since April 10

** Stock hits highest level since May 8

** Gold and copper focused miner says received firm commitments to raise A$27.5 mln ($17.64 mln) to progress near-term gold and copper production opportunity for Ravensthorpe Gold Project with processing at Forrestania

** Placement at 21 Au cents/shr

** Says placement proceeds to de-risk and accelerate the proposed acquisition of the Forrestania nickel operation

**About 1.5 mln shares change hands, vs the 30-day average of 1.3 mln

** Stock up over 110% YTD, last up 7.3%

($1 = 1.5591 Australian dollars)

data.title: Xero's Growth Supports U.S. Investment Focus — Market Talk
data.description: Xero's record organic subscriber growth in North America supports the cloud-accounting software provider's decision to increase its focus on the U.S. market, its bulls at Goldman Sachs say. They write in a note that the investment makes sense, pointing out that growth in the last fiscal year came despite a subdued performance in Canada. They see reason to be confident in Xero's fiscal 2026 revenue guidance, and like the acceleration in Xero's revenue from payments. GS lifts Ebit forecasts for the next two fiscal years to reflect stronger revenue trends, raising its target price 2% to A$205.00. GS keeps a buy rating on the stock, which is down 0.2% at A$181.69. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Taishin Financial Logs NT$4.73 Billion Profit in Q1
data.description:
Taishin Financial reported a profit attributable to owners of NT$4.73 billion, according to a Thursday filing with the Taiwan Stock Exchange.

Basic earnings per share came in at NT$0.33, while net interest revenue was NT$9.3 billion.

The insurer did not disclose year-ago figures.

data.title: Eshraq Investments Logs Lower Q1 Loss; Revenue Falls
data.description:
Eshraq Investments (ADX:ESHRAQ) said Thursday its loss for the first quarter narrowed, while revenue fell year over year.

Loss for the three months ended March 31 was 27 million Emirati dirhams, compared with 306.1 million dirhams earlier. Loss per share moved to 0.0101 dirham from 0.114 dirham.

The Abu Dhabi-listed investments and real estate company's revenue from commercial operations was 3.9 million dirhams, compared with 4.1 million dirhams a year ago.

data.title: Chang Hwa Commercial Bank Logs NT$4.13 Billion Profit in Q1
data.description:
Chang Hwa Commercial Bank reported a profit attributable to owners of NT$4.13 billion, according to a Thursday filing with the Taiwan Stock Exchange.

Basic earnings per share came in at NT$0.37, while net interest revenue was NT$6.37 billion.

The lender did not disclose year-ago figures.

data.title: Alibaba partly punctures China's AI hopes
data.description:
The $295 bln e-commerce group's shares plunged 8% in New York following tepid quarterly results. An unexpected drop in its cloud unit's profitability is worrying. Despite Alibaba's impressive AI advances, business models remain hazy and competition fierce.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow @mak_robyn on X

CONTEXT NEWS

Alibaba on May 15 reported revenue of 236.5 billion yuan ($32.8 billion) in the quarter ended March 31, an increase of 7% from a year earlier. Adjusted earnings rose 22% to 29.8 billion yuan.

Revenue from its cloud computing division rose 18% year-on-year, to 30.1 billion yuan. The company also said "AI-related product revenue maintained triple-digit year-over-year growth for the seventh consecutive quarter".

Alibaba's New York shares closed down 7.6%, to $123.90, on May 15.

data.title: Anson Resources Unit Receives Approval to Start Re-Entry Drilling at Utah Well
data.description:
Anson Resources , through its subsidiary Blackstone Minerals, secured approval to start a drilling program to re-enter the Mt Fuel-Geyser 1-25 well at its Green River project in Utah, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

The drilling program aims to extend the depth of the well and increase the thickness of the Leadville Limestone, potentially boosting the lithium-rich reservoir, the filing said.

The JORC resource estimate of contained lithium will be used in a definitive feasibility study to determine the project's economics if the program is successful, per the filing.

Shares fell 2% in afternoon trade Friday.

data.title: Air-France KLM Issues EUR500 Million Hybrid Bonds
data.description:
Air France-KLM issued 500 million euros of undated and deeply subordinated hybrid bonds, according to a Thursday release.

The bonds, set to list on Euronext Paris, carry a fixed coupon of 5.75% per annum until the first reset date. The offer received an orderbook of over 1.75 billion euros.

The European airline will use the net proceeds from the issue for general corporate purposes, including refinancing current subordinated instruments.

data.title: IMF to Release USD 1.3 Billion to Bangladesh After Reform Breakthrough
data.description:
The International Monetary Fund (IMF) will release USD 1.3 billion to Bangladesh in June following the completion of the fourth review of its USD 4.7 billion loan program, the country's finance ministry announced.

This release, covering the fourth and fifth tranches, had been delayed due to IMF demands for greater exchange rate flexibility, particularly the adoption of a crawling peg system.

The breakthrough came after April talks in Dhaka and follow-up discussions during the Bank-Fund Spring Meetings in Washington, which addressed key reforms in revenue management, fiscal policy, and the foreign exchange regime.

Bangladesh had sought IMF support in 2023 as surging global commodity prices, exacerbated by the war in Ukraine, strained its foreign reserves and import capacity.

The country has so far received USD 2.3 billion from the first three tranches.

data.title: Hily Posts Lower Q1 Attributable Profit; Revenue Up
data.description:
Hily (ADX:HH) said Thursday its profit for the first quarter decreased, while revenue grew year over year.

Profit attributable to equity holders of the company for the three months ended March 31 was 11.6 million Emirati dirhams, compared with 23 million dirhams earlier. EPS moved to 0.10 dirham from 0.19 dirham.

The Abu Dhabi-listed investment holding company's revenue from contracts with customers was 21.5 million dirhams, compared with 21.2 million dirhams a year ago.

data.title: Mitsui Chemicals studying use of ethanol to make petrochemicals
data.description:
Mitsui Chemicals is studying the use of ethanol as a feedstock in its naphtha crackers to improve competitiveness amid oversupply in the global petrochemical sector, its CEO said on Friday.

"It is difficult to completely change our conventional crackers, so we are studying to modify them to use ethanol," Hashimoto Osamu, president and CEO of Mitsui Chemicals, told Reuters on the sidelines of the Asia Petrochemical Industry Conference.

Mitsui is one of the several producers in the region looking to change its feedstock from naphtha to other fuels such as ethane as excess petrochemical capacity mainly from China has squeezed margins.

Osamu expects further consolidation in Japan's petrochemical sector by 2030 due to the challenging market conditions.

He said that petrochemical margins could recover in three to four years after new capacity additions in China slow down.

The company is also concerned about the impact of trade tariffs on Japan's exports that could affect the use of petrochemicals.

"We are waiting and watching as it is volatile…so far our customers in automotive industry are not impacted but if they are affected, our exports to the USA could get impacted," Osamu said.

data.title: Green Tea Opens Flat in Hong Kong Trading Debut
data.description:
Green Tea opened flat during its first day of trading on the Hong Kong bourse on Friday.

The casual Chinese restaurant operator offered 168,364,000 shares at HK$7.19 apiece, raising net proceeds of HK$745.8 million.

The Hong Kong portion of the offering was 317.5 times oversubscribed. As a result of the oversubscription, the company reallocated 67,345,600 shares from the international offering to the Hong Kong portion, bringing the final number of offer shares to 84,182,000 from 16,836,400.

The international portion of the offering was 7.2 times oversubscribed. Following the reallocation, the number of shares in the international offering went down to 84,182,000 from 151,527,600.

Cornerstone investors subscribed to 84,182,000 shares, or 50% of the total offer shares.

data.title: Japan's Economy Shrinks for the First Time in a Year — WSJ
data.description:
By Megumi Fujikawa

TOKYO — Japan's economy shrank for the first time in a year and faces a bumpy road ahead due to the impact of U.S. trade policy.

Japan's real gross domestic product declined 0.2% in the January-March period from the previous quarter, preliminary government data showed Friday, putting the risk of a technical recession in play.

That compared with economists' forecast for a 0.1% contraction and the 0.6% growth recorded in the October-December quarter. The economy shrank 0.7% on an annualized basis in the first quarter.

The GDP data came amid concerns that U.S. tariffs could hurt Japan's hard-earned economic recovery by weighing on exports and making companies pull back on investment.

"With the economy already shrinking on the eve of the trade war, the Bank of Japan will probably wait even longer before resuming its tightening cycle than we had anticipated," said Marcel Thieliant, head of Asia-Pacific at Capital Economics.

Thieliant said it has become increasingly difficult for the BOJ to raise interest rates in July as forecast.

Reflecting uncertainty about the global trade outlook, the BOJ recently halved its growth projection for the current fiscal year ending March 2026. While the central bank is still seeking further interest-rate increases, some economists say it might not be able to make any moves this year if trade frictions deepen.

In the first quarter, external demand, or exports minus imports, shaved 0.8 percentage point off growth, with exports down 0.6% and imports up 2.9%.

As U.S. tariffs hurt exports, one of Japan's main growth engines, persistent inflation has damaged another: private consumption, which accounts for about half of GDP. Friday's data showed that private spending was flat in the January-March period from the previous quarter.

"Continued price rises pose a downside risk to Japan's economy, affecting private consumption through a decline in consumer confidence," economy minister Ryosei Akazawa said at a news conference Friday.

Akazawa said tariff concerns haven't affected consumption yet, but the minister, who is in charge of trade negotiations with the U.S., promised to pay careful attention to consumers' mood after a recent government survey showed the possibility of worsening sentiment.

Solid corporate earnings have spurred companies to increase investments and offer pay raises. Capital expenditure was up 1.4% in the first three months of the year from the previous quarter.

Higher wages are expected to help the recovery in consumption, although food inflation has dimmed sentiment among households.

Credit Agricole economist Takuji Aida said the BOJ would need to confirm that the Japanese economy didn't deteriorate in the July-September period and update their forecasts in January before resuming rate increases.

Some economists, including Aida, expect the Japanese economy to shrink again this quarter, pushing the nation into a technical recession, defined as two consecutive quarters of contraction.

There is a chance that the second estimate of first-quarter GDP will show an upward revision, moving the economy out of the recession danger zone. That said, an upgrade isn't likely to skew the outlook too positively, said Stefan Angrick, head of Japan and frontier markets economics at Moody's Analytics.

"Coming data won't change the picture of an economy that's struggling," he said in a note.

How negotiations between Tokyo and Washington proceed is another factor to consider.

Japanese officials have been asking the Trump administration to reconsider the levies, but whether a deal will be reached remains unclear. The two sides have agreed to hold another round of discussion in mid-May or later.

So far, "2025 is stacking up to be a difficult year as tariff pain and weak domestic momentum threaten to stifle growth," said Angrick.

Write to Megumi Fujikawa at megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com

data.title: Alibaba's E-Commerce Business Monetization Likely to Continue to Improve in 2026 — Market Talk
data.description: Alibaba's e-commerce business monetization is likely to continue to improve in 2026, according to Citi analysts in a research note, after the e-commerce giant released results for the March quarter. Monetization will be supported by software fees and merchants' adoption of digital marketing tool Quanzhantui, the analysts say. Still, the misses for net profit, international commerce and logistic revenue expectations may combine to form a negative result takeaway, the analysts note. Citi analysts keep a buy rating, with a target price at HK$165.00, adding that they view Alibaba "as a proxy to China AI and macro rebound". Shares are down 5.0% at HK$122.50. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

data.title: Cambodia, US Begin Trade Talks Amid Tariff Concerns
data.description:
Cambodia has begun its first round of trade talks with the United States in Washington, aiming to negotiate a reduction in tariff rates imposed under the Trump administration.

According to a government statement on Thursday, "both sides exchanged their views in a frank and constructive manner in an atmosphere of mutual understanding on ways to further strengthen bilateral trade and investment." Talks involved Deputy Prime Minister Sun Chanthol, Commerce Minister Cham Nimul, and Sarah Ellerman, Assistant U.S. Trade Representative for Southeast Asia and the Pacific.

The U.S. is Cambodia's largest export market, and the country’s vital textiles and footwear sectors could be severely impacted by the current 49% levy rate, which is the highest among Southeast Asian nations.

A second round of discussions is expected in early June.

data.title: New Zealand Inflation Expectations Rise in May; OCR Seen Falling to 3.2% in June Quarter
data.description:
New Zealand's short- and long-term inflation expectations increased in May even as monetary policymakers are anticipated to deliver further rate cuts, according to a survey published by the Reserve Bank of New Zealand on Friday.

Expectations for one-year-ahead annual inflation grew to 2.41% from 2.15%. Two-year-ahead inflation expectations increased to 2.29% from 2.06%. Five-year-ahead and 10-year-ahead inflation expectations increased to 2.18% and 2.15%, respectively, the report said.

On average, respondents see the official cash rate declining to 3.2% by the end of the June quarter from the current 3.5%. The rate is expected to fall further to 2.91% by the end of the March 2026 quarter, according to the report.

Expectations for one-year-ahead annual wage inflation increased by 23 basis points to 2.72% from 2.49%, while the two-year counterpart slid to 2.80% from 2.83%.

data.title: Watania International Swings to Q1 Loss; Takaful Income Up
data.description:
Watania International (DFM:WATANIA) said Thursday that it swung to a loss in the first quarter, while takaful income increased year over year.

Loss after tax for three months ended March 31 was 2 million Emirati dirhams, compared with a profit of 5.4 million dirhams a year ago. Loss per share moved to 0.008 dirham from an EPS of 0.021 dirham.

The Dubai-listed investment holding company's takaful income was 184.3 million dirhams, compared with 171.4 million dirhams earlier.

data.title: UAE's Fujairah marine fuel sales hit over a year's high in April
data.description:
Marine fuel sales at the United Arab Emirates' Fujairah port reached more than a year's high in April, extending recovery for a second straight month, latest data showed.

Sales, excluding lubricants, totalled 669,378 cubic metres (about 663,000 metric tons) at the world's third largest bunker port, based on Fujairah Oil Industry Zone (FOIZ) data published by S&P Global Commodity Insights late Thursday.

Bunker volumes in April climbed 4.6% from the previous month, while edging 4.8% higher from the same month last year.

The uptick was led by supported demand for high-sulphur marine fuel, with sales climbing 12.6% month-on-month to 189,388 cubic metres.

Meanwhile, low-sulphur marine fuel sales, including low-sulphur fuel oils and marine gasoils, inched 1.8% higher to about 480,000 cubic metres.

The market share of high-sulphur bunkers widened to 28% in April, while low-sulphur bunkers were at 72%.

Bunker demand at Fujairah rebounded in March and April after a slump in February.

data.title: Xero's Customer-Value Growth Set to Drive Shares — Market Talk
data.description: Growth in the lifetime value of Xero's customers can drive material share-price gains in the next two or three years, say its bulls at Morgan Stanley. The MS analysts tell clients in a note that they see lifetime value rising from NZ$17.95 billion in the accounting software provider's last fiscal year, to NZ$21.5 billion in fiscal 2026, and to NZ$25.8 billion in fiscal 2027. They don't think that the market is fully appreciating the scale of this rise, which they see as driving equity value. Xero's rule-of-40 performance, free cash-flow, and average-revenue-per-user growth in its last fiscal year all support MS's continued overweight rating and A$220.00 target price. Shares are down 0.1% at A$181.935. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: ASX Midday Sector Update: Materials Stocks Jump, Consumer Discretionary Sector Falls
data.description:
The materials sector rose 1.65% at midday Friday, after gold prices rose more than 1%. Economic data in both Australia and the US supported market expectations of interest rate cuts.

Rio Tinto Group plans to invest $1.2 billion to modernize its Isle-Maligne hydroelectric power plant in Quebec in Canada. The modernization will support the production of low-carbon aluminium in Saguenay-Lac-Saint-Jean. The project will run until 2032.

On the other hand, the consumer discretionary sector struggled, falling 0.67%.

Light & Wonder said Caledonia Investments has become a substantial shareholder of the company on Thursday. The UK-based investment trust bank owns 8.2 million shares in the company.

data.title: U.S. tells Vietnam trade deficit 'unsustainable' and major concern during tariff talks
data.description:
The United States has told Vietnam its trade deficit with the Southeast Asian country is "unsustainable" and a major concern, Vietnamese state media reported on Friday, as the two countries hold negotiations on tariffs.

Vietnam has been holding talks with the U.S., its largest export market, to persuade the U.S. President Donald Trump's administration to take a benign view of its huge trade surplus with America, after Trump in April announced a "reciprocal" tariff rate of 46% on Vietnamese goods, to take effect in July.

Robert Kaproth, a senior U.S. Treasury official, said in a Thursday meeting with Vietnam's deputy finance minister Cao Anh Tuan the country must take measures to combat illegal transshipment and other kinds of trade fraud, Vietnam News Agency reported on Friday.

Tuan asked Kaproth to help facilitate Vietnam's imports of American high-tech products, as part of their efforts to narrow the trade deficit, which exceeded $123 billion last year.

"Vietnam has demand for products that are strengths of the United States in the fields of energy, high technology, aviation, machinery and equipment, and agricultural products," Tuan said, according to the report.

The U.S. Treasury did not immediately respond to a request for comment on the report.

In a bid to reduce its trade surplus with Washington, Hanoi has recently taken several measures, including cutting tariffs on a wide range of goods destined for the U.S. and stepped up its crackdown on Chinese goods being shipped to the U.S. via its territory.

Vietnam Prime Minister Pham Minh Chinh said the first round of tariff negotiations with the U.S. took place earlier this month, noting that the country is among the first the U.S. has agreed to hold tariff negotiations with.

Vietnam foreign ministry spokesperson Pham Thu Hang said at a regular press conference on Thursday that "Vietnam is actively promoting a balanced and sustainable trade relation with the U.S."

"Negotiations between Vietnam and the U.S. on its reciprocal tariffs on Vietnam's exports are being implemented and specific information will be provided later," she said.

data.title: Offshore Yuan Edges Higher
data.description:
The offshore yuan edged higher to around 7.21 per dollar on Friday, supported by a weakening US dollar amid soft economic figures.

The data, including an unexpected drop in producer prices and slower growth in retail sales, reinforced expectations that the Federal Reserve may implement additional rate cuts later this year.

However, the yuan’s appreciation was partially offset by growing demand for US dollar in China.

State-owned banks intervened in the onshore spot market, selling yuan and buying dollars to curb the currency’s appreciation and maintain market stability.

This intervention coincided with increased dollar demand from corporates and overseas-listed Chinese firms, many of which are preparing for dividend payouts that require foreign currency.

Meanwhile, investors now await key economic data due next week, including retail sales, unemployment rate, and updates to the loan prime rate, which could provide further insight into the health of the world’s second-largest economy.
